description_long: This guard rail consists of four vertical posts made of a material resembling wood or composite, supporting two horizontal rails that are painted white. The posts are rectangular in cross-section and are positioned at the corners of a shallow, open rectangular frame, with the rails spanning between them. The rails are mounted parallel to each other, providing a barrier that is visually open but physically restrictive. The ends of the rails are blunt and terminate flush with the posts, with no additional end treatments or safety features visible. The overall construction appears sturdy and is suitable for demarcating boundaries or preventing passage in urban environments such as parks, sidewalks, or parking lots. There are no visible reflective markings or interactive components, and the surface finish is matte with a hard, smooth texture.
description: A sturdy, rectangular guard rail with four brown posts and two parallel white rails, featuring a matte, hard surface and blunt ends. Designed for boundary demarcation in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front angle showing all four posts and both white rails, highlighting the open rectangular frame.
description_view_1: Side view emphasizing the depth of the posts and the alignment of the rails, with only two posts and the edge of the rails visible.
description_view_2: Rear angle displaying the rails from behind and the staggered arrangement of the posts.
description_view_3: Oblique rear-side view showing the rails' attachment to the posts and the blunt ends of the rails.
